name: Sam Mangwana
description:
'''Samuel Domingos Mangwana''' (born 21 February 1945), known professionally as '''Sam Mangwana''', is a [[Democratic Republic of the Congo|Congolese]]-born singer, born to Angolan parents. He sings in [[Lingala]], [[Kongo language|Kikongo]], [[Bambara language|Bambara]], [[Swahili]], [[French language|French]], English, [[Portuguese language|Portuguese]], and [[Spanish language|Spanish]]. He was the frontman of his bands Festival des Maquisards and African All Stars. Mangwana was a member of [[François Luambo Makiadi|François Luambo Makiadi's]] seminal band [[TPOK Jazz]], and [[Tabu Ley Rochereau|Tabu Ley Rochereau's]] bands [[African Fiesta]], African Fiesta National and [[Afrisa International]]. 

Mangwana used his music to address corruption and violence, and his popularity, often described as "Mangwana fever", spread across the continent from [[West Africa|West]] to [[East Africa|East]], through Central Africa, and into [[Southern Africa]].
